name: Sales Contact Properties
description: Properties extracted from sales interactions: calls, emails, meetings, and transcripts.

properties.name: Job Title
properties.description: The contact's current job title or role. Extract the most specific title mentioned (e.g., 'VP of Engineering' rather than just 'VP'). If multiple titles are mentioned, use the most recent or primary one.

properties.name: Technology Stack
properties.description: The primary technology infrastructure used by the contact's company, including programming languages (e.g., Python, Java), frameworks (e.g., React, Django), cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure), and databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB). Focus on technical stack decisions rather than product or SaaS tool usage.

properties.name: Company Size
properties.description: The approximate size of the contact's company by employee count. Infer from explicit mentions or contextual clues (e.g., 'our team of 50 engineers' suggests mid-market).

properties.name: Deal Value
properties.description: The estimated or stated monetary value of the deal in USD. Extract explicit amounts (e.g., '$450K', '450,000 dollars'). Convert shorthand: K = thousands, M = millions. If a range is given, use the midpoint.

properties.name: Deal Stage
properties.description: Current stage of the sales process with this contact. Infer from conversation context: initial outreach = Discovery, product discussion = Qualification, pricing discussed = Proposal, contract terms = Negotiation.

properties.name: Expected Close Date
properties.description: The anticipated date when the deal is expected to close. Convert relative references ('next quarter', 'by end of Q2') to ISO 8601 dates using the conversation date as reference. If only a quarter is mentioned, use the last day of that quarter.

properties.name: Pain Points
properties.description: Business problems or challenges the contact has expressed. Each pain point should be a concise phrase (5-15 words). Extract both explicitly stated problems and strongly implied ones. Examples: 'slow deployment cycles', 'vendor lock-in concerns', 'compliance audit failures'.

properties.name: Decision Maker
properties.description: Whether this contact has final purchasing authority. True if they explicitly state they can sign, approve budget, or make the decision. False if they mention needing approval from someone else. Null if unclear.

properties.name: Competitors Mentioned
properties.description: Names of competing products or companies the contact has mentioned evaluating, using, or comparing against. Include both direct competitors and alternative approaches (e.g., 'building in-house').

properties.name: Next Steps
properties.description: The agreed-upon next actions from the most recent interaction. Include who is responsible, what they will do, and any timeline mentioned. Example: 'Sarah will share the RFP by Friday; we will send API docs and schedule a technical deep-dive next week.'
